title: skopeo delete command deletes by reference even when we provide tag
body:
when we issue skopeo delete command to delete a tag instead of sending delete request by tag to image repository server skopeo sending delete by reference request. 

``` json 
skopeo --debug delete --tls-verify=false docker://localhost:8080/skopeo-delete-test:1.0.0
DEBU[0000] Credentials not found                        
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration 
DEBU[0000]  Using "default-docker" configuration        
DEBU[0000]   Using file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore  
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/localhost:8080 
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf" 
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/shortnames.conf" 
DEBU[0000] GET https://localhost:8080/v2/               
DEBU[0000] Ping https://localhost:8080/v2/ err Get "https://localhost:8080/v2/": http: server gave HTTP response to HTTPS client (&url.Error{Op:"Get", URL:"https://localhost:8080/v2/", Err:(*errors.errorString)(0xc000379d50)}) 
DEBU[0000] GET http://localhost:8080/v2/                
DEBU[0000] Ping http://localhost:8080/v2/ status 200    
DEBU[0000] GET http://localhost:8080/v2/skopeo-delete-test/manifests/1.0.0 
DEBU[0000] DELETE http://localhost:8080/v2/skopeo-delete-test/manifests/sha256:6ffade1dd9371ad14429e1564cdc189ef1fb92a44a6ddc84d5f9ef2194ce8943 
DEBU[0000] Deleting /var/lib/containers/sigstore/skopeo-delete-test@sha256=6ffade1dd9371ad14429e1564cdc189ef1fb92a44a6ddc84d5f9ef2194ce8943/signature-1
```

```skopeo --version 
skopeo version 1.4.1 commit: 130f32f047b7bf9b1fb4f95c4c5b1da6255e4829
```

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

This is the only operation that is actually possible, per the registry API specification , actual registry behavior (and past `skopeo` behavior that we can’t easily change without breaking users). Compare #489 and #795.

Somewhat recently, https://github.com/opencontainers/distribution-spec has indeed specified deleting tags, but I haven’t heard that this is widely supported (in particular https://github.com/distribution/distribution/pull/3427 is recent and haven’t been released yet); and anyway past behavior makes it difficult for Skopeo to just change what it does.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

FYI, `regctl tag rm` (see https://github.com/regclient/regclient) can remove a docker hub image tag, e.g.:

```bash
function regctl {
  docker container run --rm --net host \
    -u "$(id -u):$(id -g)" -e HOME -v $HOME:$HOME \
    -v /etc/docker/certs.d:/etc/docker/certs.d:ro \
    regclient/regctl:v0.3.8 "$@"
}
regctl tag rm $image_name:$image_tag
```

Thanks for the pointer, https://github.com/regclient/regclient/blob/8d20874d63a9dc3edc64c83663a21f8318fc0d73/regclient/tag.go#L114 is an interesting way to delete a tag on a registry that doesn’t support that.

After all [it cannot actually remove it](https://github.com/regclient/regclient/issues/96#issuecomment-930669873). It will still be there, but without any content. So effectively, no-one can actually use it, but the tag will still be there (but we can manually remove it from the registry (using the docker hub UI) when we get a chance).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Would it be possible instead try to delete by tag first and if that fails, fallback to digest?

> Would it be possible instead try to delete by tag first and if that fails, fallback to digest?

That would make it rather impossible to write a reliable script — ran against one registry it removes a tag, ran against another registry it removes the whole image and breaks unrelated deployments.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@rgl any thoughts on moving this forward?

At least on the registry side [1], we have modeled this as a configurable policy [2] so that clients are free to issue any API call they want, but the policy prevents mishaps. BTW, we can't turn this on by default since it breaks dist-spec [3] conformance.

References:
[1] https://github.com/project-zot/zot
[2] https://github.com/project-zot/zot/pull/951
[3] https://github.com/opencontainers/distribution-spec
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

DEBU[0000] Returning credentials for localhost:32094/nginx from DockerAuthConfig

https://zotregistry.dev/v2.1.0/developer-guide/api-user-guide/#oci-endpoints

/v2/{repo}/blobs/{digest} | DELETE, GET, HEAD | Lists or deletes an image's blob/layer given a digest
-- | -- | --

skopeo delete  zot container storage
only delete a tag, the blob data already exists

add an option to rm the image's blob?

https://zotregistry.dev/v2.1.0/articles/storage/#configurable-attributes

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c3142f61-d86b-4687-b2b6-bc611921ae2f)
the GC will reclaim the orphaned blobs!!

I have a workflow that:

When i run `delete imageName:tag`, it can delete only that tag.
At least I think it was successfully because garbage-collection can mark somethings to be deleted. (I didn't check if the hash are all matched.)
Then i checked my whole registry via joxit registry UI, tags are correct.
When i deleted all tags, files still in `docker/registry/v2/repositories`.
I can directly delete files manually and run garbage-collection again.

Can my words be an advice?
Of course maybe you don't want or have no permission to delete files directly on registry's filesystem, then forget what i said.
